cases.passage: The concept of peace is crucial for the development and progress of any nation. In 2015, the United Nations adopted the 2030 Agenda for Sustainable Development, which includes 17 Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) to be achieved by 2030. The third SDG is 'Ensure Peace, Justice, and Strong Institutions.' This goal aims to promote peaceful and inclusive societies for sustainable development, provide access to justice for all, and build effective, accountable, and inclusive institutions at all levels. The goal also aims to reduce violence and combat crime and trafficking, and promote the rule of law and human rights. The implementation of this goal requires cooperation and collaboration among governments, civil society, and other stakeholders.

questions.q: What is the third Sustainable Development Goal (SDG) as per the 2030 Agenda for Sustainable Development?
opts: Ensure Poverty Reduction
Ensure Peace, Justice, and Strong Institutions
Ensure Quality Education
Ensure Good Health and Well-being
questions.explanation: The third SDG is 'Ensure Peace, Justice, and Strong Institutions'.

questions.q: What is the primary objective of ensuring access to justice for all as per the third SDG?
opts: To promote peaceful and inclusive societies
To provide access to justice for all
To reduce violence and combat crime and trafficking
To promote the rule of law and human rights
questions.explanation: The primary objective of ensuring access to justice for all is to provide access to justice for all.

questions.q: Who are the key stakeholders involved in the implementation of the third SDG?
opts: Only governments
Only civil society
Governments, civil society, and other stakeholders
Only international organizations
questions.explanation: The key stakeholders involved in the implementation of the third SDG are governments, civil society, and other stakeholders.

questions.q: What is the target year for achieving the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) as per the 2030 Agenda for Sustainable Development?
questions.explanation: The target year for achieving the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) is 2030.

questions.q: What is the aim of the third SDG to reduce violence and combat crime and trafficking?
opts: To promote peaceful and inclusive societies
To provide access to justice for all
To promote the rule of law and human rights
To reduce violence and combat crime and trafficking
questions.explanation: The aim of the third SDG to reduce violence and combat crime and trafficking is to reduce violence and combat crime and trafficking.

cases.passage: The concept of peace is a complex and multifaceted idea that involves the absence of war, violence, and conflict. In 1993, the United Nations established the United Nations Peacebuilding Commission (UNPC) to help countries recover from conflict and build sustainable peace. The UNPC works with governments, civil society, and other stakeholders to support peacebuilding efforts in post-conflict countries. The commission's work focuses on four key areas: disarmament, demobilization, and reintegration (DDR); security sector reform; justice and rule of law; and economic recovery and development. The UNPC's goal is to help countries build sustainable peace and prevent the recurrence of conflict.

questions.q: What is the primary objective of the United Nations Peacebuilding Commission (UNPC)?
opts: To prevent the recurrence of conflict
To support peacebuilding efforts in post-conflict countries
To promote disarmament, demobilization, and reintegration (DDR)
To promote economic recovery and development
questions.explanation: The primary objective of the UNPC is to support peacebuilding efforts in post-conflict countries.

questions.q: What is the focus of the UNPC's work on economic recovery and development?
opts: To promote security sector reform
To promote justice and rule of law
To promote economic recovery and development
To promote disarmament, demobilization, and reintegration (DDR)
questions.explanation: The focus of the UNPC's work on economic recovery and development is to promote economic recovery and development.

questions.q: What is the United Nations Peacebuilding Commission (UNPC) established for?
opts: To promote disarmament, demobilization, and reintegration (DDR)
To promote security sector reform
To help countries recover from conflict and build sustainable peace
To promote economic recovery and development
questions.explanation: The UNPC is established to help countries recover from conflict and build sustainable peace.

questions.q: In which year was the United Nations Peacebuilding Commission (UNPC) established?
What is the focus of the UNPC's work on security sector reform?
opts: To promote disarmament, demobilization, and reintegration (DDR)
To promote justice and rule of law
To promote security sector reform
To promote economic recovery and development
questions.explanation: The focus of the UNPC's work on security sector reform is to promote security sector reform.
